pi_items.source: File analysis of export_result.sh and setup_task.sh, verified against task.json description
File analysis of export_result.sh, verified against task.json description
Task description states 'Select the 3 most prominent/clear comets to analyze'; verifier.py checks row_count >= 3

pi_items.verified_value: Synthetic image generated by ImageMagick (fallback when download fails). Claimed source URL https://github.com/bravo-group/OpenComet/raw/master/samples/1.tif returns 404 Not Found.
pi_items.source: Web search verified that BravoGroup/OpenComet repository does not exist; actual OpenComet is at github.com/bgyori/opencomet without a samples folder. Direct URL test via wget returned 404 Not Found error. Fallback synthetic image generation code present in setup_task.sh.

privileged_info_summary: The task involves comet assay image analysis using Fiji ImageJ. The expected CSV output is at ~/Fiji_Data/results/comet/comet_analysis.csv with 3 or more comet measurements. The expected PNG output is at ~/Fiji_Data/results/comet/roi_overlay.png showing ROI overlays. The metadata claims data comes from 'OpenComet Repository (samples/1.tif)', but this source repository (bravo-group/OpenComet) does not exist on GitHub (returns 404). The actual OpenComet software is maintained by bgyori at github.com/bgyori/opencomet and does not contain public sample files. The task will use a synthetic comet image generated by ImageMagick as a fallback when the primary download fails.
